response:
**Not yet implemented** (deliberately scoped to the decision itself, per "don't fix a judgment call before deciding it"): the CLAUDE.md bullet, the new hook script + test, and filing the foreground-wait lesson as its own GitHub issue (ADR 0021) — this repo has no remote/`gh` access in this sandbox, so that issue can't actually be filed here; it should be created once you have GitHub access. These are the natural next steps if you want me to carry them out.

---

title: "Sync-before-spend: CLAUDE.md bullet + PostToolUse reminder hook; defer only the abort gate"
status: accepted
summary: "Issue #30 ran in full while main already carried the merged fix (~$5-8, ISSUE-106.md:6): clone fresh-at-start, stale-by-use. Fix = a sync-before-spend bullet in CLAUDE.md's Shipping section PLUS a non-blocking PostToolUse hook mirroring retrospect-reminder.sh, firing on every costly dispatch command — prose alone doesn't survive context compaction (red-team). Reject the claim-protocol (permission-gated no-op). Defer only a blocking PreToolUse abort gate — higher-effort, one caught incident."
---

# 0045 — Sync-before-spend: CLAUDE.md bullet + PostToolUse reminder hook; defer only the abort gate

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer, session-operator, process-economist — two-sided (opposing counsel + a cost lens); red-team then broke the first draft's "prose is enough" framing. plugin-adopter omitted: no downstream-consumer surface.
- Context: a backlog session executed issue #30 in full (~$5-8 nested `claude -p`) while main already carried the merged fix (PR #97); caught only at end-of-session `git fetch` — clone fresh-at-start, stale-BY-USE mid-session (ISSUE-106.md:6). Nothing forces a re-sync + re-read of issue/PR state immediately before spending (ISSUE-106.md:10).

## Decision
1. **Sync-before-spend bullet** in CLAUDE.md's Shipping section, beside the git-fetch/rebase rule (CLAUDE.md:52-54): before the first paid `claude -p`/agent dispatch on a backlog issue, and again immediately before final push — `git fetch origin main`, re-read the issue, search merged PRs referencing it, stop if closed/merged. Human-readable spec, not the enforcement.
2. **Ship a non-blocking PostToolUse hook now** (`pdca-workflow/hooks/`, mirrors `retrospect-reminder.sh`'s Bash-matcher pattern + its test): substring-match `claude -p` in `tool_input.command`, inject `additionalContext` nudging re-fetch/re-check. Fires on every costly dispatch call regardless of context compaction — red-team (Break 2) showed the bullet alone lacks this property; needs no issue-write permission (answers Break 3's asymmetry vs. the claim-protocol).
3. **Reject the claim-protocol** (issue-comment "in progress", ISSUE-106.md:15) — needs issue-write permission dispatch "sometimes withhold[s]"; #2's hook is what makes this rejection non-self-defeating (Justification).
4. **Defer only a blocking PreToolUse abort gate** — higher-effort (parse args, abort semantics, false-abort risk); one caught incident. NOT "defer all mechanization" (first draft's error).
5. Dispatch-prompt-as-step-1 (no such template exists in-repo, grep-confirmed) and the foreground-wait lesson (ISSUE-106.md:18) are separate → revisit trigger / own GitHub issue (ADR 0021), not decided here.

## Justification
Red-team broke two load-bearing claims in the first draft: (a) "boundary-anchored prose survives compaction" — nothing re-injects CLAUDE.md at the `claude -p` boundary in a compacted session; (b) "no clean spend-gate entry point exists" over-generalized from "no abort gate" to "no entry point," when `hooks.json` + `retrospect-reminder.sh` (+ test) already prove a cheap, tested PostToolUse matcher reusable for `claude -p`. That hook costs no more than the existing one and buys compaction-proof, permission-free enforcement the bullet can't — closing both breaks without a blocking gate. The abort gate stays deferred: narrower, real added risk (false aborts, decision-logic-test burden) one incident doesn't justify. Poka-yoke: the hook prevents; the bullet only documents intent.

## Assumptions
- [verified] a PostToolUse Bash-matcher entry point already exists, cheaply: `hooks.json:3-14` wires `retrospect-reminder.sh`, which extracts `tool_input.command` and substring-matches (`:14-17`); `retrospect-reminder.test.mjs` tests it. Same shape matches `claude -p` — read 2026-07-14, corrects the prior draft's "no entry point" overclaim.
- [checkable] the new hook, once written, passes a test analogous to `retrospect-reminder.test.mjs` and never blocks a call — owner: verifier at implementation.
- [unverifiable] parallel-dispatch collisions are rare (1 in repo history) — undetected duplicates leave no trace (caught only by luck, ISSUE-106.md:6), so this is survivorship-limited; why the abort gate stays deferred. REOPEN-IF a second collision is caught/inferred despite the hook firing → build the abort gate, with a decision-logic test.
- [unverifiable] whether boundary (a) would have caught #30 specifically — needs `origin` + GitHub history unreachable in this sandbox; the command shape mirrors CLAUDE.md:53's pattern, not a new mechanism. REOPEN-IF external reproduction finds a timing gap → tighten the boundary.
- [checkable-doc] CLAUDE.md Shipping section (:46-72) exists; its git-fetch/rebase bullet (:52-54) is the sibling anchor — PM verified 2026-07-14.

## Rejected alternatives
- Prose-only, no hook (first draft) — doesn't survive compaction; indistinguishable in enforcement from the claim-protocol it rejects.
- Claim-protocol / "Both" (ISSUE-106.md:15-16) — permission-gated no-op in the riskiest sessions; superseded by the hook, which needs no issue-write permission.
- A blocking abort gate now — real added risk (false aborts, untested-logic debt) not yet justified by one incident; the hook captures most value at a fraction of the risk.

## Revisit triggers
- A second collision (caught/inferred) despite the reminder firing → build the blocking abort gate, with a decision-logic test (CLAUDE.md Never).
- A backlog-dispatch prompt template surfaces in-repo → add this bullet as its step 1; reconsider the claim-protocol if issue-write is default-granted.
- The hook ships but a collision slips through unnudged → the matcher is too narrow; widen it.

---
name: session-operator
description: Daily Claude Code operator — live-session execution-realism advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the SESSION OPERATOR for this project — an engineer who runs Claude Code sessions against
this plugin every day and has watched agents misread rules that looked crisp on paper.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): will an LLM agent in a
live session actually execute this rule as intended — is the routing decidable in the moment,
what gets misclassified under ambiguity, and where does an agent rationalize the cheap path
(tier-shopping, skipped gates)?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

Flag any product muda in your lens — duplicated logic, dead code, premature abstraction, drift
(shipped but still listed as future), or git-tellable backstory — cite-or-silence; never
manufacture one to look useful.

Recommending a platform/vendor feature or asserting a vendor/product name, model number, or
domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.
